Coach Report — Weeks 6-19 + Current Update

Tyron Samaroo  |  OCB Contest Prep  |  Historical report through May 10 + manual update through May 22, 2026
Coach report read

Wk 19 is a calorie spike plus low-step week; review the pattern before digging through meals.

Weight is -3.86 lb from Wk 6 to Wk 19, with the lowest checkpoint at Wk 18. The latest week combines 2,335 average calories, 5,126 average steps, and 40 unique foods, so the useful question is whether the spike came from meal timing, heavier meal slots, or template drift.

Open Meal Patterns Review Daily Meals Compare Recent 4
Scale path 154.5 → 150.7 Net -3.86 lb; low was Wk 18 at 148.50 lb.
Calories 2,335 Wk 19 avg, +756 vs Wk 18.
Steps 5,126 Wk 19 avg, -5,722 vs Wk 18.
Food template 40 20 new foods; 28 staples across the report.
Current update

Wk20 is an intentional diet-break gap; Wk21 is partial cleanup data.

The historical MacroFactor report still ends at Wk 19. This section adds the local manual ledger so the coach view does not misread the staycation or miss the current cleanup week.

Manual ledger

Wk20 / May 11-17: staycation diet break

Calories were intentionally untracked during the staycation/diet break. This should be excluded from macro averages and compliance scoring, not treated as zero intake or a failed logging week.

Training also had a real break: multiple gym days off, roughly six by your note. Exact count can be tightened later if the workout export catches up, but the coach-facing read is clear: this was a chosen reset, not missing adherence data.

StatusExcluded
ReasonDiet break

Wk21 / May 18-22: cleanup runway, partial current data

Manual rows show the aggressive cleanup after the staycation. Food/macros are verified locally through May 21; May 22 has a weight-only feedback row so far.

Scale move165.0 -> 158.4
Wk21 delta-6.6 lb
Avg intake986 cal
Avg protein176g
Avg steps13,464
Adjusted active1,424 kcal

From the May 17 primer weight, the cleanup ledger is -10.6 lb through the May 22 weight-only row. Treat this mostly as water/glycogen/gut cleanup plus some fat loss, not a settled new baseline.

Heatmap
Which meal slot carried the calorie load? Wk 19 had 6 meals over 650 calories. The biggest single load was Meal 5 on Tue 05-05 at 1816 calories. Open calorie heatmap
Timing
Did the eating window drift? Wk 19 averaged 2.9 meals per day across a 8.3 hour window, so the timing view should be checked before reading every food row. Open meal timing
Protein
Were anchors consistent enough? 4/7 days hit 165g protein. There were 13 40g+ anchor meals and 1 meals under 25g. Open protein split
Template
Is this drift or a stable template? Latest week logged 40 unique foods with 20 new additions. Across the report, 28 foods qualify as staples. Open template evolution

What changed in Wk 19

Fuel moved upAverage calories moved +756 from Wk 18; 2/7 days cleared 1,900 calories.
Output moved downSteps moved -5,722 from Wk 18; this makes the latest calorie average harder to judge from scale weight alone.
Meal load6 meals crossed 650 calories. The heaviest was Meal 5 on Tue 05-05: 1816 cal / 87P.
Protein spread4/7 days hit 165g protein; 13 meals were 40g+ anchors and 1 meals were under 25g.

Fast review order

1. HeatmapFind which meal slots carried the Wk 19 calorie load instead of reading every food row first.
2. TimingCheck late meals and drift; Wk 19 averaged 2.9 meals/day over a 8.3 hour eating window.
3. TemplateUse Template Evolution for the longer cut signal: 28 staples, 19 regulars, 160 one-off or rare foods.
4. Daily detailDrop into Daily Meals only after the analysis view tells you which day or meal slot is worth inspecting.
Daily detail scope Showing Wk20/Wk21 current update first. Wk20 is excluded; Wk21 is partial manual data.
WEEK 20 — 05-11 – 05-17
Staycation / intentional diet break | calories excluded | multiple gym days off | not scored as compliance failure

Wk20 daily detail: intentionally untracked

This week is now in the Daily Meals surface because it is part of the report timeline, but it should not show fake meals. Calories were intentionally untracked during the staycation/diet break, so macro averages, heatmaps, protein splits, and compliance scoring should exclude it.

Coach readDiet break
Macro statusExcluded
TrainingDeload/off
Next usable rowMay 17

May 17 is kept as the primer row for the cleanup runway: 169.0 lb, 873 cal, 169P, legs 56:16, short walk.
